title: Trying to run podman in distrobox container gives: 'uid_map failed: Operation not permitted'
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to run podman in a distrobox container on Fedora Silverblue and the container won't start.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Per the instructions at https://github.com/89luca89/distrobox/blob/main/docs/useful_tips.md#using-podman-inside-a-distrobox, I create a distrobox container for Fedora 40 with the `--unshare-all --additional-packages "podman crun"` arguments:

```
distrobox create -i registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-toolbox:40 --name fedora-40 --hostname f40 --unshare-all --additional-packages "podman crun"
```

Then after entering the container with:
```
distrobox enter fedora-40
```
Based on the distrobox documentation, I run:
```
sudo usermod --add-subuids 10000-65536 $USER
sudo usermod --add-subgids 10000-65536 $USER
cat << EOF | sudo tee /etc/containers/containers.conf
[containers]
netns="host"
userns="host"
ipcns="host"
utsns="host"
cgroupns="host"
log_driver = "k8s-file"
[engine]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
events_logger="file"
EOF
```

Then, when I try and run a container with podman inside the distrobox container, it fails with:
```
$ podman run busybox
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 2800 0 1000 1 1 10000 55537`: newuidmap: write to uid_map failed: Operation not permitted 
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 2802 0 1000 1 1 10000 55537`: newuidmap: write to uid_map failed: Operation not permitted 
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 2804 0 1000 1 1 10000 55537`: newuidmap: write to uid_map failed: Operation not permitted 
ERRO[0000] invalid internal status, try resetting the pause process with "podman system migrate": cannot set up namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": should have setuid or have filecaps setuid: exit status 1
```

### podman info output

Podman 5.1.1

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

comments.body:
> /usr/bin/newuidmap": should have setuid or have filecaps setuid

Did you check that? `getcap /usr/bin/newuidmap`, `ls -l /usr/bin/newuidmap`

Yes you need CAP_SETUID/CAP_SETGID for this to work.  This would be blocked if the newuidmap does not have the filecaps set or if the process running Podman did not have the capabilities within its bounding set.  (Usually happens when running podman within a confined space like a container.

After chmod +s on newuidmap and newgidmap the error changes to:
```
$  podman --log-level debug run busybox
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called run.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level debug run busybox) 
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"              
INFO[0000] Using sqlite as database backend             
DEBU[0000] systemd-logind: dial unix /var/run/dbus/system_bus_socket: connect: no such file or directory 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /var/home/tmds/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /run/user/1000/containers     
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /var/home/tmds/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /var/home/tmds/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Using transient store: false                 
DEBU[0000] Not configuring container store              
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend file              
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime crun-wasm initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun-wasm: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runc: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime krun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime krun: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime ocijail initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime ocijail: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime crun-vm initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun-vm: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runj initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runj: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime youki initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime youki: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Using OCI runtime "/usr/bin/crun"            
INFO[0000] Setting parallel job count to 37             
DEBU[0000] Could not move to subcgroup: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/init: permission denied 
ERRO[0000] invalid internal status, try resetting the pause process with "podman system migrate": could not find any running process: no such process 
```
Trying to run the `podman system migrate` segfaults:
```
$ podman system migrate
stopped 10d473d9aad799c9b1d31f8e62e40e48775942d0fee527a085166b5752bea32c
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0x55ec3a7fee9b]
...
```
Do you have your home dir and run dir (/run/user/$UID) shared with the host? This would be a very bad idea as you now have two different podman insistences acting on the same storage.

> Do you have your home dir and run dir (/run/user/$UID) shared with the host? This would be a very bad idea as you now have two different podman insistences acting on the same storage.

It seems that this is what distrobox has set up for me.

I'm trying out Silverblue. I've set up my development environment in a distrobox container, and now I want to run a test suite that involves spinning up containers with podman. I'm trying to find out how I can make that work.

Based on what @Luap99 said, I think the _podman in distrobox_ scenario is unsupported.

I did get my test suite to work by allowing the distrobox container to use the host podman executable as described in https://github.com/89luca89/distrobox/blob/main/docs/useful_tips.md#using-hosts-podman-or-docker-inside-a-distrobox.
